summary: Completed comprehensive testing of TrustTrade escrow app for fee_paid_by field fix. Backend API fix applied (added fee_paid_by to Transaction Pydantic model). All 8 backend tests pass. Frontend tests pass - logo visible, fee split default is 50/50, fee agreement warning displays correctly.

fixes_applied.issue: Transaction Pydantic model was missing fee_paid_by field, causing field not to be returned in API responses
fixes_applied.fix: Added 'fee_paid_by: str = "split"' to Transaction model at line 76

issues: React hydration errors in table components (<tr> inside <span>)
design_issues.note: This is a pre-existing issue from iteration_2 and does not block functionality

passed_tests: ✅ Landing page loads with logo (64x64px) properly sized
✅ Dashboard loads with logo visible after authentication
✅ Auth endpoint /api/auth/me returns 401 for invalid tokens
✅ Auth endpoint /api/auth/me returns 200 with user data for valid tokens
✅ Transaction creation /api/transactions returns 201 with fee_paid_by field
✅ Buyer pays fee option works in API
✅ Seller pays fee option works in API
✅ New Transaction page shows fee split selector with default 'Split 50/50 (Recommended)'
✅ Fee agreement warning message displayed: 'Escrow fee option must be agreed by both parties before payment'
✅ Price breakdown shows correct 2% fee calculation
✅ Price breakdown shows buyer pays and seller receives with 50/50 split
✅ Currency properly displayed in South African Rand (R)
seed_data_creation: Created test user with user_id: test-user-1772733063533 and session_token: test_session_1772733063533
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend fix applied to Transaction model to include fee_paid_by field. All transaction-related tests pass. React hydration errors still exist in dashboard table components but don't affect functionality. Authentication uses Emergent-managed Google OAuth - test sessions can be created via mongosh.

mocked_apis.note: Email sending is MOCKED via mock_send_email function in server.py

rca_of_the_issue.root_cause: The Transaction Pydantic model (response model) was missing the fee_paid_by field, while the TransactionCreate model (input model) had it. This caused the API to accept and store fee_paid_by correctly in MongoDB but not return it in API responses.
reproduction_steps: 1. Create a transaction via POST /api/transactions with fee_paid_by field
2. Check response - fee_paid_by field was missing from response JSON
3. The data was saved correctly in MongoDB but not serialized back to client
rca_of_the_issue.mitigation: Added 'fee_paid_by: str = "split"' field to the Transaction model (response model) at line 76 in server.py
